title: Scite-grounded Citicoline enrichment pilot
review_notes: This proposal does not alter evidence_tier, evidence_grade, profile_status, or publishing controls.
The dosage field describes regimens studied in human trials and is not individualized advice.
Positive memory findings are framed as preliminary and population-specific because study populations, doses, outcomes, and designs are heterogeneous.
The 12-week 500 mg/day trial used a branded citicoline ingredient; avoid generalizing one product-specific trial to all products.

sources.title: Citicoline and Memory Function in Healthy Older Adults: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
Is Citicoline Effective in Preventing and Slowing Down Dementia?—A Systematic Review and a Meta-Analysis
Citicoline Efficiency on Cognitive Function: A Systematic Review
sources.authors: Cano-Cuenca, Solís-García del Pozo, and Jordán
sources.journal: Journal of Aging Research and Lifestyle

sources.title: Citicoline: A Superior Form of Choline?
The Role of Citicoline in Neuroprotection and Neurorepair in Ischemic Stroke

changes.expected_old_value: Citicoline (CDP-choline) is a choline source studied as non-stimulant support for attention and focus, and for cognition in older adults. Human evidence is limited and the effects are modest — it is a supportive option rather than a dramatic cognitive enhancer, and works best alongside sleep and healthy habits.
changes.new_value: Citicoline (CDP-choline) supplies choline and cytidine-derived precursors used in phosphatidylcholine metabolism. Small randomized trials in selected older adults suggest possible short-term improvements in memory, including a 12-week study using 500 mg/day, but the broader evidence is heterogeneous and does not establish a large or universal cognitive-enhancing effect. Human studies generally report good short-term tolerability.
changes.rationale: Adds mechanism context, a specific human-trial anchor, and explicit limits against overgeneralizing heterogeneous cognition evidence.

changes.new_value: Human evidence includes small randomized placebo-controlled trials and systematic reviews across healthy older adults and people with cognitive impairment. A 12-week trial of 500 mg/day reported improvements in episodic and composite memory versus placebo, while reviews describe heterogeneous populations, outcomes, and study quality. Neutral findings in other neurological indications limit broad generalization.
changes.rationale: Explains why the evidence should remain limited rather than upgrading the evidence tier from one favorable trial.

changes.new_value: Most favorable cognition findings come from short-term studies in selected older adults or cognitively impaired populations rather than large independent trials in broadly healthy adults. Doses, indications, comparators, and outcome measures vary, so findings are best treated as suggestive and population-specific.
changes.rationale: Captures the key external-validity and heterogeneity limitations visible across the trial literature.

changes.new_value: Oral research regimens commonly use 500–1,000 mg/day for approximately 4–12 weeks. These are studied regimens, not individualized dosing advice.
changes.rationale: Adds a bounded description of human research regimens while explicitly avoiding a personal recommendation.
